name: stop_device_control
description: Kill switch for remote device control: immediately STOPS control_device sessions on the user's phone/tablet/XR device over Tailscale, halting a runaway or unwanted run. Use this the instant the user says 'stop', 'stop controlling my phone', or 'cancel that'. By default it kills ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the resolved device; pass a task_id to kill just that one. Operator-scoped.

device.description: Optional target device name (tailnet hostname or MagicDNS name). Omit to use origin-aware routing: the device the request came from, else the operator's designated PRIMARY device.

task_id.description: Optional specific device-control task id to kill (as returned in a control_device run). Omit to kill ALL in-flight device-control tasks for this operator on the target device.

returns: A confirmation of the kill, plus data.killed_count (how many in-flight tasks were aborted), data.device, and — on failure — a structured error_kind (no_device / no_primary_device / invalid_target / origin_mismatch / lost_contact / refused / bad_response).
notes: M8.2 of the frontier-driven device-control rewire: reaches the phone's RemoteControlServer POST /kill-all (or POST /kill/{task_id}) over Tailscale, which calls RemoteSessionBus.stopAll(operator) / stop(taskId) on-device (the killed task is refused on every subsequent /action + /stream frame, and its consent banner drops). Operator-scoped + tailnet-gated exactly like /action. Device resolution is origin-aware (mesh.resolve_device), the same rule control_device uses. Also reachable as control_device(action="stop"). See docs/plans/2026-06-30-android-control-frontier-driven-plan.md (M8).
